commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 14. "From His prepared habitation:" from His habitation of assumed Humanity, which He prepared for Himself. "He looketh upon all the inhabitants of the earth" (ver. 14): He looketh mercifully upon all who live in the flesh, that He may be over them in ruling them.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Prepared. St. Jerome, "from his most established throne "whence he beholds all the conduct of men, (Calmet) though he fill all places, and work in all. (Berthier)
His power and wisdom (ver. 15.) are infinite. (Worthington)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
